reasoning: Query requests Tohoku cherry-blossom spots in Spanish, explicitly excluding Tokyo. Tool fetched only Akita (one of six Tohoku prefectures). RULE C: authoritative data returned but region scope is severely incomplete — Akita-only covers ~17% of Tohoku. No sakura-specific filter applied; top results are a castle ruin and a volcanic lake. Constraint failure on both region (Tohoku→Akita only) and sakura relevance.
missing_data: Aomori Hirosaki-koen, Miyagi Tsuruoka, Iwate Kitakami, Fukushima Miharu Takizakura — all top Tohoku sakura spots absent. No sakura_meisho_100 filtering.
improvement_hint: For Tohoku queries, fan-out across all 6 Tohoku prefectures and filter by kinds containing sakura_meisho_100; or use search_hybrid with region expansion.

reasoning: Query asks for major Niigata sake breweries in English. Response returns 24 items from MAFF GI + Wikidata sake brands/breweries. Top entries include Koshi no Kanbai, Hakkaisan, Kubota — the canonical 越の三梅 brands. Well-structured with QIDs, authorities, descriptions. ONE caveat: response mixes sake brands (not breweries per se) with GI foods like Kurosaki Chamame edamame, diluting brewery focus.
missing_data: Brewery physical addresses and visit info (tours) absent; Kakurei, Dassai-adjacent brands sparse.
improvement_hint: Add category sub-filter for sake_brewery kind_tag to separate brands from general food GIs; include brewery visit info when available.

reasoning: Query in French requests ski resorts with hot springs in Hakuba. Tool returned get_spots for 白馬 in Nagano, which is correct geographically. Response includes Hakuba Happo-One ski resort (relevant) but no onsen/hot-spring venues. RULE D: query is in French, response data in Japanese/English — constraint_handling -1 from otherwise-deserved 3. Primary failure: no onsen venues returned despite being a dual-intent query.
missing_data: Hakuba onsen facilities (Hakuba Happo-One Onsen, Mimizuku no Yu, Hakuba Highland Hotel onsen) entirely absent. No thermal-spring kind filter applied.
improvement_hint: For dual-intent queries (ski+onsen), call get_hotels with onsen_ryokan filter OR filter get_spots by onsen/hot_spring kinds in addition to ski_resort.

reasoning: Query asks for best autumn foliage spots across Kansai region. Tool fetched only Kyoto (one of seven Kansai prefectures). RULE C: authoritative data but severe scope gap — only Kyoto returned for a multi-prefecture Kansai query. Response lacks any foliage-season filtering (no koyo kinds) and does not cover Osaka, Hyogo, Nara, Shiga, Mie, Wakayama. Constraint_handling 2 for region mismatch.
missing_data: Nara Yoshino autumn, Osaka Minoh Falls, Hyogo Nunobiki Herb Garden, Shiga Eikan-do equivalent sites all absent. No koyo kind filter.
improvement_hint: Fan-out across all 7 Kansai prefectures for region queries; apply kinds filter for koyo/autumn_foliage to surface seasonally relevant spots.

reasoning: Query in Arabic asks for famous Kansai cherry-blossom spots. Tool fetched Nara only (one Kansai prefecture). RULE D: query in Arabic, response in Japanese/English — constraint_handling -1. Nara is partially valid (吉野山 with sakura_meisho_100 is the top result), but Kansai scope requires at minimum Osaka Maruyama, Kyoto Maruyama, and Himeji. RULE B: single root cause = F (constraint: region scope + language).
missing_data: Osaka Kema Sakuranomiya, Kyoto Maruyama Park, Himeji Castle — canonical Kansai sakura sites absent.
improvement_hint: Fan-out to all Kansai prefectures filtering sakura_meisho_100; return Arabic descriptions where available via get_description.

reasoning: Query asks for stargazing spots on Okinawa remote islands (離島). Tool fetched all Okinawa prefecture spots. Top results are castles and monuments in Naha — no remote island or stargazing/dark-sky content at all. RULE C: authoritative data but entirely wrong sub-scope (mainland Okinawa vs.离島) and no dark_sky kind. Intent understanding 2 as off-topical dominates.
missing_data: Iriomote-jima, Taketomi-jima, Yonaguni dark-sky spots; no dark_sky_100 or astro-tourism kind entries at all.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky_100 / astro-tourism kind filter; for remote-island queries restrict to municipality codes for specific islands (Iriomote, Taketomi, Yonaguni).

reasoning: Query in Thai requests rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido. Tool returned get_hotels(Hokkaido). Response leads with 日本秘湯を守る会 (Nihon Hito-yu) ryokan — excellent match for rural onsen. However: RULE D — query in Thai, response in Japanese/English only, so constraint_handling -1 from otherwise-deserved 3=2. Rural intent partially met by hito_yu_kai entries but urban hotels like ANA Holiday Inn Sapporo dilute relevance.
missing_data: Thai-language descriptions absent. Rural filtering not applied — urban hotels mixed in. Coordinates missing for hito_yu_kai entries.
improvement_hint: Filter lodging_type to onsen_ryokan when query includes onsen; use municipality to exclude major cities for rural queries; ensure Thai translation pipeline covers hotel descriptions.

reasoning: Query in Vietnamese asks for Seto Inland Sea islands worth visiting. Tool fetched Kagawa spots — mostly mainlandspots (Ritsurin Garden, Mannō Lake, Marugame Castle). No island-specific results. Seto Inland Sea spans Kagawa, Hiroshima, Okayama, Ehime. RULE C: authoritative data but wrong sub-scope — islands need island kind filter and multi-prefecture expansion. Vietnamese query unaddressed (RULE D sub-issue).
missing_data: Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima, Omishima, Ikuchijima — canonical Seto island destinations absent. No island kind filter.
improvement_hint: Fan-out to Hiroshima/Okayama/Ehime and filter by island kind; Naoshima data should be present via Kagawa municipal scrape.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Tool correctly called get_local_specialty craft in Tokushima. Response returns Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave (indigo-related textile) and Awa Washi (indigo-dyed washi) — relevant. However, no dedicated indigo-dyeing workshop/venue data returned. RULE C: authoritative METI designation data present but query was about experiential workshops, not product designations. Intent partially met.
missing_data: Actual workshop venues (e.g., Aizenkobo workshop Tokushima, Studio Buaisou) absent — no venue/address data for hands-on experiences.
improvement_hint: Add workshop/experience venue kind_tag; supplement METI craft designations with municipal scrape data for experiential tourism.

reasoning: Query in German asks for temple lodgings (Shukubo) on Mt. Koya. Tool fetched get_hotels(Wakayama). Response leads with 高野山宿坊協会 member temples: Annyoin, Ichijoin, Ekoin, Kitamuroin — exactly matching intent. Designation clearly states Koyasan Shukubo Association. Well-structured with phone numbers and Japanese descriptions. ONE caveat: no coordinates, no English descriptions for shukubo entries, and no German translations.
missing_data: English/German descriptions for shukubo entries; coordinates absent for all shukubo; booking URL / website missing for most.
improvement_hint: Add coordinates for shukubo entries via geocoding; generate English descriptions for shukubo; consider adding German translation for high-tourism destinations.

reasoning: Query asks for off-the-beaten-path samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area(武家屋敷) returns 153 matches including Kakunodate (Akita), Matsue Buke Yashiki, Uchiyama Family Residence (Fukui) — good coverage of non-Kyoto options. RULE E not applicable (no featured_cluster). ONE caveat: Tokyo 武家屋敷門 appears second in results which is arguably 'beaten path', but excludes Kyoto successfully.
missing_data: Kitsuki (Oita) samurai district, Chiran (Kagoshima) samurai gardens — lesser-known options not appearing in top results.
improvement_hint: Apply negative prefecture filter to exclude Kyoto and Tokyo for 'outside X' queries; boost preservation_district + buke_yashiki combined hits for off-beaten-path framing.

reasoning: Query in Indonesian asks for summer festivals in Akita prefecture. Tool returned get_festivals(Akita) with lang=id. Response contains only 3 items — all 重要無形民俗文化財 (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) designations: Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shinmei Festival. These are cultural properties, not summer festival events with dates. The famous Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり) and Oga Namahage are entirely absent. Count=3 is sparse for a prefecture with major summer matsuri.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり, August), Omagari National Fireworks (covered in L2-06 data but not here), Yokote Kamakura — all major Akita summer events missing.
improvement_hint: Expand festival data sources beyond bunka_intangible to include scraped_schema_event and hanabi kinds; summer festivals need event date filtering for June-August.

reasoning: Query in Korean asks for small fishing port towns in the San'in region. Tool fetched Shimane spots. Response leads with Lake Shinji, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan — all prominent but none are fishing port towns. San'in region spans Shimane and Tottori; 漁港 (fishing port) intent completely unaddressed. No fishing port or coastal town kind entries visible.
missing_data: Hamada (漁港), Misumi (fishing village), Tottori Karo coast fishing towns — no port/harbor kind filter applied. Tottori prefecture entirely absent.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_port / gyoko kind; fan-out to Tottori for San'in queries; filter by coastal/maritime kinds for port town queries.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi paper making in Gifu. Tool called get_local_specialty craft in Gifu. Response returns 8 items including Mino Ware (ceramics), Hida Shunkei (lacquer), Nagiso Lathe Work — but washi-specific entries are not visible in the truncated output. The query is experiential ('where can I experience') and METI designations don't provide venue/workshop data. RULE C: authoritative craft data present but experiential intent not met.
missing_data: 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi) METI designation may appear later in the 8 items but workshop venues (Mino City Udatsu Washi Museum, Warabi Paper Mill) absent.
improvement_hint: Surface workshop_venue kind alongside METI craft designations; ensure Mino Washi appears as first result when washi is the query keyword via BM25 boosting.

reasoning: Query in Spanish asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Tool fetched all Ishikawa prefecture spots. Response leads with Kanazawa Castle and Kenroku-en — city landmarks, not Noto Peninsula fishing villages. The Noto Peninsula is a distinct sub-region of Ishikawa. No municipality filter applied for Noto. No coastal/fishing town kind filter applied.
missing_data: Wajima (輪島), Suzu, Noto-cho fishing villages entirely absent. No noto_peninsula municipality scoping. Machiya/funaya (boat houses) content missing.
improvement_hint: Support sub-regional queries within prefectures; for Noto Peninsula, scope to northern Ishikawa municipalities (Wajima, Suzu, Noto-cho); add fishing_port/funaya kind.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes not just Tottori. search_area(砂丘) returns 91 results including Tottori (expected) plus Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari sand dunes museum (Hokkaido), Fukiagehama (Kagoshima), Tsujido Beach (Kanagawa) — good multi-prefecture coverage of sand_dune kind. ONE caveat: Tottori砂丘 appears twice (duplicate entries from BUNKA and wikidata sources), and a municipal scrape noise entry for Tottori film promotion appears. Top results still useful.
missing_data: Sarobetsu Dunes (Hokkaido), Nakatajima Dunes (Shizuoka) — major non-Tottori dunes potentially deeper in 91 results.
improvement_hint: Deduplicate sand_dune entities across wikidata and heritage sources; add a Tottori-exclusion hint when query explicitly says 'not just Tottori'.

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks for traditional crafts of the San'in region. Tool called get_local_specialty craft Shimane in zh. Response returns 4 items (Iwami Ware, Sekishu Washi, Unshū Soroban, Izumo Stone Lanterns) all in Chinese — correct language. RULE C: Shimane only; San'in includes Tottori. Count=4 is modest but legitimate for Shimane. Failure G for coverage: Tottori crafts (Inshū Washi, Tottori mingei) absent.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture crafts missing — Inshū Washi, Tottori Folk Crafts. San'in region should span both Shimane and Tottori.
improvement_hint: For region-name queries (山陰), auto-expand to both Shimane and Tottori; add region_fanout logic for San'in as a named region.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields beyond Furano in Hokkaido. Tool fetched all Hokkaido spots. Response leads with Shōwa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle — no lavender content at all. The query has explicit sub-constraint ('beyond Furano') and a specific kind (lavender fields). No lavender kind filter applied; Furano itself might have lavender kind but it's buried.
missing_data: Biei (青い池 area lavender), Nakafurano, Sapporo Takino Suzuran Hillside Park lavender gardens — all absent. No lavender kind filter.
improvement_hint: Add lavender_field / flower_garden kind with seasonal metadata; for 'beyond X' queries, allow explicit exclusion of a named spot while expanding to similar kinds across the prefecture.

reasoning: Query in Filipino asks for traditional old towns of Kyushu. Tool fetched Oita spots. Response returns castles and shrines — legitimate Oita content, but Oita alone doesn't cover Kyushu old towns. The query intent is 古い町並み (historic townscapes) across Kyushu, which spans 7 prefectures. RULE C: authoritative data but wrong scope — single prefecture for 7-prefecture region. Also: 'traditional towns of coffee' (Tagalog: 'bayan ng kape') may be mistranslation by query author meaning 'old towns'; either way, Oita-only is insufficient.
missing_data: Nagasaki Glover Garden/Dejima, Kagoshima Chiran historic district, Kumamoto Yamaga, Yanagawa canal town — major Kyushu historic townscapes absent.
improvement_hint: Fan-out across all Kyushu prefectures for region-level queries; filter by preservation_district + jokamachi kinds for historic townscape intent.

reasoning: Query asks for stayable kominka (traditional farmhouses) in Hokuriku. Tool fetched get_hotels(Ishikawa). Response contains onsen ryokan and business hotels — no kominka lodging at all. Hokuriku spans Ishikawa, Fukui, Toyama; Ishikawa only is partial. No kominka/machiya lodging_type in the dataset surfaced.
missing_data: Kominka guesthouses in Fukui, Toyama, Ishikawa Noto area absent. No kominka / machiya lodging_type filter. Shirakawa-go (Gifu border) gassho-zukuri farmhouses not mentioned.
improvement_hint: Add kominka / machiya lodging_type; fan-out to all Hokuriku prefectures for region queries; consider flagging gassho-zukuri heritage properties as bookable kominka where data supports.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines of the Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage(Wakayama) returns Kongōbu-ji, Kumano Kodō, Kumano Sanzan with full descriptions and UNESCO WHS designations — exactly matching mountain sacred site intent. tool_scope_note correctly advises on UNESCO vs Japan Heritage distinction. ONE caveat: no Yoshino/Kinpusen-ji (Nara side of Kii), and some heritage_designations_labels contain raw QIDs instead of resolved strings (Q11633166 unreesolved).
missing_data: Nara side of Kii Peninsula (Yoshino Kinpusen-ji, Tamaki-jinja) not covered as tool is Wakayama-scoped.
wrong_data: heritage_designations_labels shows 'Q11633166' unresolved QID instead of a human-readable label.
improvement_hint: Resolve all QIDs to labels in heritage_designations_labels; for Kii Peninsula queries, fan-out to Nara and Mie in addition to Wakayama.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast. Tool returned get_festivals(Niigata, en) with 129 items. The Nagaoka Fireworks Festival (長岡まつり大花火大会) — one of Japan's top 3 fireworks events — appears as item 3, which is good. However, the first two results are a phlox festival and a lily festival — neither are fireworks. Sea of Japan coast spans multiple prefectures; only Niigata fetched. RULE B: fireworks data present but buried below non-fireworks results.
missing_data: Other Sea of Japan fireworks (Akita Omagari, Toyama Bay fireworks) absent — Niigata-only scope. Dedicated hanabi kind filter not applied.
improvement_hint: Filter get_festivals by event kinds including hanabi/fireworks when query explicitly requests fireworks; for coastal-region queries fan-out to adjacent prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. Tool correctly called get_local_specialty craft Oita in English. Response includes Beppu Bamboo Crafts (別府竹細工) with full METI designation, detailed description of bamboo types and 8 weaving techniques, and production area. This is a direct match. Also includes Hakata Ori textile which is cross-prefectural. see_also_wikidata_venues adds museum links. ONE caveat: count=2 items, Hakata Ori is not bamboo-specific.
missing_data: Workshop visit info for Beppu Bamboo Craft Museum absent; Oita 竹工芸 festival data not linked.
improvement_hint: Link METI craft designations to nearby museum/workshop venues where coordinates available; separate single-prefecture items from cross-prefectural ones in display order.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Tool fetched Ehime spots. Response includes Ōmishima island (cycling_route + island kinds) — a relevant entry. However Shikoku spans 4 prefectures; Ehime only is partial. The Shimanami Kaidō cycling route (spanning Ehime/Hiroshima) is implicitly present via Ōmishima. No dedicated cycling route filter applied beyond what appears in kinds. Coverage is sparse for a 4-prefecture region.
missing_data: Kochi Shimanto River cycling, Kagawa Shikoku cycling paths, Tokushima cycling routes absent. No cycling_route kind filter cross-prefecture applied.
improvement_hint: Fan-out to all 4 Shikoku prefectures; filter by cycling_route kind to surface dedicated cycling infrastructure across the island.

reasoning: Query asks where to see traditional kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. Tool returned get_traditional_arts(en) with 183 items. Response leads with Bunraku (puppet theatre), then Kabuki as UNESCO ICH — but these are art-form designations, not venue/location data. No geographic venue information for kabuki performances outside Tokyo/Kyoto. RULE C: authoritative UNESCO/intangible data present but synthesised incorrectly for a venue-finding query. Subsequent items (Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku) are irrelevant to kabuki.
missing_data: Konpira Grand Theatre (Kotohira, Kagawa) — Japan's oldest kabuki theatre; Chichibu Yo-matsuri kabuki; regional kabuki venue addresses entirely absent.
improvement_hint: For venue-seeking kabuki queries, supplement get_traditional_arts with get_spots filtered by kabuki_theater kind; add Konpira Grand Theatre as a landmark entity with venue data.
